title: trifolium dasyphyllum subsp. anemophilum
intro: Trifolium dasyphyllum subsp. anemophilum, also known as the sticky clover, is a subspecies of the Trifolium dasyphyllum plant, a type of perennial herb native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The sticky clover is also known to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
